examples.title: Industry Consent Flow under Environmental Laws
examples.description: Process flow for obtaining consent from Pollution Control Board
examples.code:
PROCEDURE: ObtainIndustryConsent
BEGIN
 1. Industry prepares application with:
 - Details of manufacturing processes
 - Pollution control measures
 - Effluent treatment facility plans
 - Environmental impact assessment (if required)
 
 2. Submit application to State Pollution Control Board
 
 3. PC Board conducts site inspection
 
 4. IF inspection satisfactory THEN
 Grant Consent to Establish (CTE)
 ELSE
 Issue deficiency notice
 ENDIF
 
 5. After construction, apply for Consent to Operate (CTO)
 
 6. IF compliance verified THEN
 Grant CTO (valid for 5-10 years)
 ELSE
 Show cause notice / Closure order
 ENDIF
 
 7. Industry must submit annual compliance reports
 
 8. Regular monitoring by PC Board officials
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode represents the procedural flow that industries must follow to obtain necessary consents from Pollution Control Boards under Indian environmental laws (Water Act and Air Act).

examples.title: EPR Target Calculation for Producers
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate recycling targets under Extended Producer Responsibility
examples.code:
PROCEDURE: CalculateEPRTarget
INPUT: ProducerID, SalesData[], CollectionData[], Year

BEGIN
 // Step 1: Calculate sales in the current year minus 10% overlap
 CurrentYearSales = GetSalesData(ProducerID, Year)
 PreviousYearSales = GetSalesData(ProducerID, Year-1)
 
 // Step 2: Apply collection formula
 CollectionTarget = (CurrentYearSales * 0.3) + (PreviousYearSales * 0.1)
 
 // Step 3: Calculate recycling target (60% of collection target)
 RecyclingTarget = CollectionTarget * 0.6
 
 // Step 4: Compare with actual collection
 ActualCollection = Sum(CollectionData)
 
 // Step 5: Determine compliance status
 IF ActualCollection >= CollectionTarget THEN
 Status = "COMPLIANT"
 Shortfall = 0
 ELSE
 Status = "NON-COMPLIANT"
 Shortfall = CollectionTarget - ActualCollection
 ENDIF
 
 OUTPUT: CollectionTarget, RecyclingTarget, Status, Shortfall
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how Extended Producer Responsibility targets are calculated for E-waste management, showing how producers must meet collection and recycling obligations based on their sales.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - Dependent on sales data size
